articles.title: Is laughter really the best medicine?
articles.description: Researchers launch a "Laughter Lab" to explore how laughing can improve wellbeing and health.

articles.title: There is a significant risk of Ebola spillover into South Sudan, WHO study warns
articles.description: There is a 70% chance that Ebola could reach South Sudan, according to a World Health Organization modelling study published on Thursday. Researchers said that preparing the country for a proper response is the “most urgent priority.”

articles.title: Europe’s heatwave is the hottest and most humid ever
articles.description: The current temperatures in western and central Europe would have been virtually impossible 50 years ago, and unprecedented humidity levels make this heatwave especially dangerous

articles.title: New MSP's extraordinary journey from being 'bedbound' to being elected to Holyrood
articles.description: Liberal Democrat plans to campaign for better diagnosis and treatment of Lyme disease and other chronic illnesses

articles.title: UK's new national supercomputer might be even smarter than Douglas Adams' Deep Thought
articles.description: The UK’s new national supercomputer will be astonishingly powerful, but will it be able to work out the answer to the ultimate question?

articles.title: SNP halt plans for new £2.1bn Monklands Hospital over financial woes
articles.description: Health Secretary Angela Constance said the current plans would be more expensive than building both the Queen Elizabeth University Hospital and HMP Glasgow.

articles.title: Hong Kong IPO market's edge lies in its strong aftermarket performance: Ion Analytics
articles.description: Ion Analytics' Romaine Jackson says Hong Kong IPO demand is being driven by increasingly selective investors focusing on areas such as AI infrastructure, semiconductors and computing power, supporting strong aftermarket performance.

articles.title: Apple shares slide as Mac and iPad hikes raise fears of higher iPhone prices
articles.description: CNBC’s MacKenzie Sigalos reports on why Mac and iPad increases are being read as a warning that higher memory and storage costs could eventually hit the iPhone.

articles.title: Concerns grow over accelerating spread of Ebola in Congo: 1,155 cases and 304 deaths
articles.description: Health authorities are reporting a new increase in cases over the past 24 hours, while the outbreak of the rare Budibugi strain continues to spread The post Concerns grow over accelerating spread of Ebola in Congo: 1,155 cases and 304 deaths appeared first on ProtoThema English .

articles.title: 'People gave us belief': Dubai family thanks UAE community after new cancer treatment begins
articles.description: Dubai teacher begins new cancer treatment in Germany, as UAE community support gives her family hope and belief for the long journey ahead.

articles.title: Some in MAHA movement express frustration over Supreme Court ruling on Roundup weed killer
articles.description: One member of the “Make America Healthy Again” movement said the decision was "sickening" after the Supreme Court ruled that Bayer, the manufacturer of Roundup, did not need to warn consumers of a potential cancer risk associated with its weed killer. NBC News’ Julie Tsirkin reports on the MAHA movement's frustration with the Supreme Court's ruling.

articles.title: Insurance increasingly dropping weight loss drugs like Wegovy, Zepbound
articles.description: More and more employees are losing insurance coverage for weight loss drugs like Wegovy and Zepbound, which may change where customers go to get the drugs.

articles.title: Exercise, not liraglutide alone, drives vascular gains after obesity-related weight loss
articles.description: A prespecified secondary analysis of the S-LiTE trial found that regular exercise reduced carotid artery wall thickness and circulating levels of inflammatory cytokines in adults with obesity who had already achieved substantial diet-induced weight loss. Liraglutide alone did not significantly improve these vascular or inflammatory markers, while exercise plus liraglutide also improved endothelial function biomarkers.

articles.title: Scientists find a universal gut microbiome pattern linked to colorectal cancer
articles.description: Researchers analyzed 6,779 stool microbiome profiles and 906 tissue samples to identify a colorectal cancer microbial signature that remained consistent across age groups, populations, and sequencing methods. The signature aligned with tumor-resident microbes, showed weaker detection for adenomas, and was inversely associated with dietary fiber intake.

articles.title: Beating the winter blues: Prevent and treat seasonal depression
articles.description: Psychologist Sandy Rae shares tips on how to avoid the winter blues, also known as 'SAD'.

articles.title: New Zealand's secret weapon that gives them the edge over England in the extreme heat - and why both sets of players are suffering behind the scenes
articles.description: RICHARD GIBSON: The weather that greeted the start of the 68th such contest here on the banks of the River Trent was inhospitable with the mercury peaking at 32 degrees.

articles.title: Tearful Bella Hadid sparks health fears as she reveals debilitating 'flare up' that's left her 'out of breath' and 'fainting'
articles.description: Bella Hadid sparked new health fears as she revealed a debilitating Lyme disease 'flare up' amid her years-long battle with the illness.

articles.title: Controversial billionaire tax will appear on November ballot
articles.description: Proponents of a controversial billionaire tax pledge to making sure it appears on the November ballot to backfill federal healthcare funds.

articles.title: Trump nominates Chris Klomp as HHS second-in-command
articles.description: President Donald Trump on Thursday named Medicare director and former health care executive Chris Klomp to be deputy secretary of the US Department of Health and Human Services.

articles.title: Ashley Tisdale's Go-To Face Cream Is 'Absolute Magic' for Sensitive Skin
articles.description: Few skincare concerns are as frustrating as dryness. Finding the right moisturizer can be tough, but Ashley Tisdale is letting fans in on the moisturizing cream she swears by. The Illiyoon Ceramide Ato Concentrate Cream is a multi-use formula designed for dry, sensitive skin and those with a compromised skin barrier. Tisdale praised the formula [...]

articles.title: Trump nominates new top deputy to RFK Jr.
articles.description: Chris Klomp, a 45-year-old tech entrepreneur, gained the president's confidence when he negotiated price cuts with drug companies.

articles.title: June brings on ALS Awareness Month
articles.description: Doctors say awareness is crucial, especially for early diagnosis and access to care.

articles.title: Serious statin side effects on muscles are extremely rare, new research confirms
articles.description: People are more worried about severe muscular problems when taking statins than they should be: These side effects are exceedingly rare, research published Thursday in the journal The Lancet Digital Health reaffirms.

articles.title: ‘Not acceptable’: Ontario pharmacist sounds alarm on drug shortages
articles.description: It has been a frustrating few weeks for Kyro Maseh behind the counter of his Beaches pharmacy. Patients are showing up with prescriptions for common drugs to treat glaucoma, post-surgery pain, and heart burn. Those medications are backordered and in many cases the alternatives are hard to come by with no clear answers about when stock will return to normal.

articles.title: Opinion: Red light therapy didn’t go viral by accident
articles.description: The wellness craze may overpromise, but its popularity reveals how badly people want relief from burnout.

articles.title: US Supreme Court sides with Bayer, blocking thousands of Roundup cancer lawsuits
articles.description: The US Supreme Court on Thursday sided with Bayer in a ruling expected to block thousands of lawsuits claiming its Roundup weedkiller caused cancer by failing to carry adequate warning labels. The decision hands a victory to President Donald Trump's administration but drew backlash from allies in the "Make America Healthy Again" movement.

articles.title: This autism therapy is covered by many insurers. Why does the military’s plan restrict it?
articles.description: TRICARE, the military’s healthcare program, limits coverage of applied behavior analysis, a type of therapy for people with autism. Kristi Cabiao is trying to get the policy changed.

articles.title: The Science of Relationships Is Moving Beyond Date Night and Toward Shared Experiences
articles.description: Novel activities together may be more effective than routine date nights.

articles.title: Final Trade: NVO, RRC, XLE, FCX
articles.description: CNBC’s “Fast Money” team shares their final trades of the day.

articles.title: Amazon not yet oversold on intermediate-term basis: Fairlead Strategies' Stockton
articles.description: CNBC’s “Fast Money” team discusses Amazon’s recent stock performance and what investors should be watching with Katie Stockton, founder and managing partner of Fairlead Strategies.

articles.title: Methuen birthing center’s early closure sparks nurses concern for patients health
articles.description: Merrimack Health Methuen Hospital's birthing center will close its doors on June 29 and move patients to Merrimack Health Lawrence Hospital.

articles.title: Bayer wins in Roundup cancer case in the Supreme Court
articles.description: The Supreme Court ruled in favor of Bayer on Thursday in its fight against lawsuits accusing the company of not warning of the cancer risks of the weedkiller Roundup and its chemical glyphosate.

articles.title: Dementia Risk Could Be Reduced by Daytime Light Exposure
articles.description: Bright daylight could lower dementia risk more than other major risk factors, new 8-year study has found.

articles.title: Medical Journal Retracts Study Claiming Cancer Therapy Is More Effective When Given in the Morning
articles.description: In a notice flagging a series of problems with a clinical trial, the journal Nature Medicine said its editors “no longer have confidence in the integrity of the results.”

articles.title: Dan Ives: Apple had to raise prices and this was the right time
articles.description: Dan Ives, Wedbush Securities, joins 'Closing Bell' to discuss Apple's latest price hikes, why the company decided to make the decision now and much more.

articles.title: ‘Paradigm-shifting’ treatment for pediatric diabetes cleared by FDA
articles.description: Yale’s Kevan Herold was the senior investigator on a clinical trial which showed that the drug teplizumab — recently approved for pediatric patients with Stage 3 Type 1 diabetes — delays the progression of the disease.

articles.title: Pour Fragrance Bar in College Hill lets you create a custom scent that's uniquely yours
articles.description: If you've ever sprayed a perfume or lit a candle and wished it smelled a little more like you, a shop in College Hill is making that possible.

articles.title: Is “Salt Stomping” the Key to Better Sleep, Reduced Stress, and Achieving an Overall Calm?
articles.description: Experts say this wellness ritual does work, but there’s a catch

articles.title: STAT+: At BIO 2026, industry wrestled with Washington politics, and making AI work better
articles.description: Biotech executives reveal concerns over Chinese biotech, the profitability of AI, and the durability of Trump's drug price moves.

articles.title: Dementia Will Cost the U.S. $800 Billion in 2026 Alone
articles.description: The estimated total factors in often-overlooked burdens like unpaid caregiving and family-wide earning potential loss.

articles.title: Mom Thought Toddler Had Stomach Bug, Then They Were Rushed to ICU
articles.description: Erin Dimond said her one-year-old daughter Marlee's illness started out as just a "runny nose" before escalating to surgery.

articles.title: Three courageous heart transplant survivors are celebrated by the British Heart Foundation for giving their old hearts for research
articles.description: The British Heart Foundation is celebrating three incredible young women who donated their old hearts to science after having heart transplant surgery.

articles.title: Supreme Court shuts down cancer lawsuits over Roundup weedkiller
articles.description: The Supreme Court ruled that the maker of the weedkiller Roundup can't be sued for failing to warn that the popular product might cause cancer.

articles.title: Micron soars on blowout quarter: AI boom fuels memory chip demand
articles.description: CNBC’s Kristina Partsinevelos joins 'Squawk on the Street' with the latest news from Micron and Qualcomm.

articles.title: Medicare to launch GLP-1s for $50: Here's what to know
articles.description: CNBC’s Angelica Peebles reports on the latest news.

articles.title: Lack of clean cooking fuel and electricity threatens global health, well-being
articles.description: At a time when energy security and affordability have risen to the top of the development agenda, 655 million people globally still lack access to electricity, and two billion use polluting fuels and technologies for cooking putting their health and well-being at risk.

articles.title: Newsletter exclusive: Are restless legs driving you crazy in the heatwave? My GP didn't know how to help - then a top neurologist prescribed me a very unlikely but common drug
articles.description: I'm pregnant - and have restless legs syndrome (RLS), a common disorder described by the NHS as an overwhelming urge to move your legs to stop an uncomfortable sensation.

articles.title: ABC to air Taylor Frankie Paul's season of 'The Bachelorette' in mid-July: Sources
articles.description: ABC plans to air in mid-July the latest season of "The Bachelorette" featuring Taylor Frankie Paul, which the network suspended this year over domestic violence incidents involving the reality TV personality, news outlets report, citing production sources.

articles.title: Cardiac nurse explains how people with heart conditions can stay safe in heatwave
articles.description: A cardiology expert has outlined a series of tips for how people with heart problems can stay safe during the heatwave

articles.title: Unexpected Ways to Strengthen Relationships With Family, Friends
articles.description: Studies show that it's the quality of our relationships with others that is the biggest predictor of our personal well-being. Psychiatrist Dr. Samantha Boardman joins TODAY with some unexpected ways to strengthen those relationships, including implementing the Michelangelo Effect.
